# Plan: Add `--version` flag to the `awm` CLI

## Context
The `awm` binary has no way to report its own version. Users and scripts
expect `awm --version` to print the build version. The crate version is
already defined (`0.1.0`, inherited from `[workspace.package]` in the root
`Cargo.toml`), so it only needs to be surfaced at runtime — no dependency or
manifest change is required.

## File to edit
- `crates/awm/src/main.rs` — the only file changed.

## The one function to change
`main()` (`crates/awm/src/main.rs:390-446`). Args are parsed by hand via
`std::env::args()`; there is no `clap`. Add a single early-return block right
after the args are collected (line 391), before the `--demo` check at line 393,
mirroring the existing `args.iter().any(|a| a == "...")` mode-flag pattern:

```rust
if args.iter().any(|a| a == "--version" || a == "-V") {
    println!("awm {}", env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"));
    return Ok(());
}
```

Notes:
- `env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION")` resolves to the workspace version at build time;
  the codebase already uses `env!(...)` (e.g. `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` at line 297).
- `main` returns `std::io::Result<()>`, so `return Ok(())` is consistent.
- Do not confuse this with `claude_version()` (lines 714-723), which shells out
  to `claude --version` and is unrelated.

## Verification
- `cargo build && cargo test` (per CLAUDE.md, before committing).
- `cargo run -p awm -- --version` prints `awm 0.1.0`.
- `cargo run -p awm -- -V` prints the same.
- Confirm existing flags still work (e.g. `cargo run -p awm -- --demo`) since the
  new block returns early only on the version flag.
